reviews.review_text: Obviously, this is not my real name to protect my privacy. Everyone here is usually courteous and efficient. My only 2 concerns is that this place is run by none-doctors like Physician Assistants and Nurse practitioners. I am sure they have a supervising physician somewhere, but the many time my family has visited, we have never seen one.   This type of provider works for most urgent situations but not all.  My other concern is that the PA that saw me flat out refused to send my prescription to my desired and preferred pharmacy, Amazon, with no reasonable explanation. If they had used my pharmacy I would have my medication by now because they deliver same day or next day. But even using a local pharmacy, I have difficulty getting there on time, so it has been 3 days and I still don't have my meds. Of course my mobility issues are not Baptist's fault, but the refusal to use my pharmacy of choice certainly is.  Hope things improve because there are not much other choices in the island.

reviews.review_text:
Dear Hospital Administrator,

I hope this letter finds you well, though I cannot say the same for myself after the harrowing experience I underwent at your Emergency Room (ER) on Tuesday, October 11, 2023.

Upon my arrival at approximately 9:10 PM with alarming chest pain and a severe headache, I was met with an appalling lack of urgency and concern from the registration staff. Despite my visibly deteriorating condition, the registration process was not only painfully slow but also utterly insensitive to my urgent medical needs.

Post-registration, I and several other patients, some of whom were persistently coughing, were left unattended in the outdoor waiting area. This exposed us to potential cross-infections and created a stressful environment without immediate medical assistance in sight.

An agonizing hour later, I was ushered into a room for vital signs checking, then sent back to the lobby. This pattern of neglect continued as I was shuttled in and out of rooms for various tests, including blood work and X-rays, with lengthy, unexplained waiting periods in the lobby between each procedure.

During the blood work process, the attending nurse mishandled the needle, causing significant bruising and added distress on my end. The lack of professionalism and skill exhibited during this critical procedure was unacceptable.

Adding to the series of mismanagement, I learned that the scan machine vital for my diagnosis was non-operational. This not only delayed the diagnostic process further but left me in prolonged discomfort and uncertainty about my medical condition.

Around midnight, after nearly three hours of distressing experience, I was finally assigned a room. However, the uncaring and dismissive attitude persisted with the registration and nursing staff. Despite five hours since my arrival, no nurse practitioner or physician had evaluated my precarious health condition, leaving me feeling neglected and anxious.

Considering the seriousness of my symptoms, the substantial delay and lack of proper medical attention could have had fatal consequences. The thought of patients in urgent need being left unattended and unassessed in such a chaotic environment is both disheartening and alarming.

I urgently request an immediate and thorough investigation into this matter and appropriate corrective actions to prevent future patients from undergoing similar ordeals. A prompt and detailed response addressing my grievances and outlining the steps taken to amend the issues identified during my visit is expected and imperative.

Your immediate attention and action regarding this serious matter are anticipated and much needed.

Yours sincerely,

Gerardo

We understand that you have been in contact with our Patient Experience team, but if you would like to discuss any other concerns with one of our representatives, please feel free to contact our team at SMPatientExperience@baptisthealth.net or 786-662-5046. Thank you.
